title: Normalize post-terminal handling into the canonical no-active baseline
goal: Ensure that once a transaction reaches a terminal outcome, runtime behavior and materialized state converge to the canonical no-active baseline without leaving any ambiguity about active resumability.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 plan requires explicit terminal completion, non-terminal commit semantics, and structural no-active handling. This ticket makes terminal completion operationally final by normalizing post-terminal runtime state into a clear no-active baseline while preserving historical durability through canonical event history and any documented terminal snapshot handling.
scope: Review all runtime code paths that execute after terminal completion (`done` or `blocked`).
Identify where materialized state, in-memory state, or helper responses continue to expose completed work as active or resumable.
Define the exact normalization path from terminal completion to the canonical no-active baseline.
Ensure the canonical no-active baseline is structurally represented with `active_tx: null` and `status: null`.
Ensure `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null` once materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline.
Ensure `last_applied_seq` is retained according to the plan when available.
Ensure resume logic treats terminal transactions as non-active work and does not attempt to continue them.
Preserve historical durability through canonical event history and any documented terminal snapshot handling without letting that handling redefine active resumability.
out_of_scope: Broad compatibility rewrites unrelated to post-terminal behavior.
inputs: Outputs from p0-t01, p0-t02, and p0-t03
Any runtime modules that update materialized transaction state after terminal lifecycle events
Any runtime modules that decide whether a transaction is active or resumable
expected_outputs: A code-level description of all post-terminal normalization paths.
An implementation update that guarantees terminal transactions are not left exposed as active resumable work.
A clear mapping from terminal completion to the canonical no-active baseline.
A short implementation note describing any retained terminal snapshot handling and why it does not redefine active resumability.

canonical_no_active_baseline.last_applied_seq: retains the last materialized event sequence when available

rules: After terminal completion, the transaction is no longer active.
Materialized state must not continue to expose completed work as resumable active work.
Once materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline, `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null`.
Resume logic must not treat terminal transactions as active work even if historical or snapshot data remains readable.
Terminal durability must be preserved by canonical event history and any documented terminal snapshot handling, but such handling must not reintroduce active resumability.
acceptance_criteria: A transaction that has ended as `done` or `blocked` is never treated as active resumable work by runtime continuation logic.
Post-terminal runtime behavior converges to the canonical no-active baseline defined in the plan.
The implementation does not leave `active_tx` populated in any state that is still interpreted as resumable after terminal completion.
Once the canonical no-active baseline is reached, `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null`.
The implementation preserves historical durability without allowing terminal snapshot handling to blur the difference between historical visibility and active resumability.
Repeated encounters with terminal transactions do not reopen work or corrupt lifecycle state.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that terminal completion paths cannot accidentally fall through into resume dispatch.
Confirm that any materialized terminal snapshot handling is distinguishable from active resumable state.
Confirm that helper responses and machine-readable workflow guidance no longer advertise terminal work as active.
Confirm that no-active baseline handling remains consistent with the minimal canonical continuation contract.
notes: This ticket is the runtime counterpart of the plan's requirement that terminal completion be explicit and distinct from commit.
This ticket must preserve the structural no-active model introduced elsewhere and must not reintroduce sentinel active-transaction semantics.
If current runtime behavior briefly retains terminal snapshots, the implementation must still guarantee that such snapshots are not interpreted as active resumable work.
